title: Indie Rethink Run
objective: Survive a 2-4 minute run through escalating hazard waves, cashing in risky Rethink flips to post the highest leaderboard score before your three Hearts are gone.

controls.outcome: Your spark snaps into a neighboring lane to avoid incoming blocks, walls, and shockwaves.
You stay steady, useful for threading narrow gaps and setting up multiplier routes.

controls.input: Swipe into a glowing Rethink Gate
controls.outcome: For a few seconds the rules invert: marked hazards become bonus targets to graze, while empty lanes become dangerous.

steps: Start with three Hearts, a x1 multiplier, and slow descending waves of 'stale idea' blocks.
Swipe between three to five vertical lanes as hazards fall faster and patterns get tighter every 20 seconds.
Graze near hazards to build the Indie meter; close calls are worth more but a direct hit costs one Heart.
When the Indie meter fills, a Rethink Gate appears in a risky lane.
Enter the gate to start Rethink Mode for 4 seconds: bright icon hazards are now score-rich graze targets, but unmarked space and old safe-hh
Exit Rethink Mode back into normal dodging as wave speed, lane count, and mixed patterns escalate.
The run ends when all Hearts are lost or a full collapse wave pins you with no legal lane.
scoring: Score rises from survival time, near-miss grazes, and chained Rethink Mode bonuses. Each clean graze adds combo; entering a gate at high combo boosts the multiplier, but taking a hit resets it. Local offline leaderboard:
tips: Build Indie meter with controlled near-misses instead of playing fully safe.
Do not auto-take every gate; a bad entry angle can turn the flip into a trap.
Use Rethink Mode to harvest marked hazards, then reposition before normal rules return.
Audio helps: a rising synth warns of gate spawn, and a filter-swap signals the rule inversion instantly.

trendContext.title: Indie games that made me rethink what gaming could be
